title: Structs 2
description: Rust also has tuple structs (unnamed fields) and unit structs (no fields at all).
instructions: Create a tuple struct and a unit struct, then instantiate them.
hints: Tuple structs have unnamed fields, defined as: struct Name(Type, Type, ...);
The fields are accessed by index: .0, .1, .2 etc.
Add three i32 fields: struct ColorTupleStruct(i32, i32, i32);
explanation: Tuple structs have fields without names, accessed by index. They are useful when you want to give a tuple a name and make it a different type from other tuples. Unit structs have no fields at all and are useful for implementing traits on a type without storing data. The `#[derive(Debug)]` attribute lets you print structs with `{:?}`.
